commentators.name: Mike Wilson
commentators.key_quote: While we might see some more choppiness in coming weeks, our conviction in the current bull market is intact.
key_views: Bull market remains intact despite recent pullback.
Correction underscores a normal shift in an earnings-driven bull market.
New investment opportunities emerging amid S&P 500 pullback.

commentators.name: Tom Lee
commentators.key_quote: The S&P 500 will reach new all-time highs before entering a bear market later in 2026.
key_views: S&P 500 to reach new highs before a 2026 bear market.
Anticipates a rise in U.S. equities in the coming weeks.
Oil prices benefit the U.S. economy as an oil exporter.
commentators.price_target_or_view: 7,700 by end of 2026 (from other sources), 7,300 before potential March decline.

commentators.name: Jurrien Timmer
commentators.key_quote: Markets are being pulled in two directions, with growth trends on one side and valuation pressures on the other.
key_views: Strong earnings growth and rising profit margins support the market.
Higher inflation and interest rates create valuation pressures.
AI advancements contribute to productivity improvements.
commentators.price_target_or_view: Complex market backdrop due to competing forces.

commentators.name: Liz Ann Sonders
commentators.key_quote: The bull market is still alive, earnings are strong, and the economy has held up better than many expected.
key_views: Bull market remains alive with strong earnings.
Economy has shown resilience.
Beneath the positives, there are fault lines to consider.
commentators.price_target_or_view: Nuanced message, bull market alive but with underlying concerns.

commentators.name: Mohamed El-Erian
commentators.key_quote: The U.S. economy faces a looming capital crunch as corporations and governments prepare to tap markets for massive amounts of funding.
key_views: Sticky inflationary pressures persist.
Potential for a rate hike by year-end.
Uncertainty about capital availability for funding needs.
commentators.price_target_or_view: Complex macroeconomic landscape driven by divergent forces.

commentators.name: Jeremy Siegel
commentators.key_quote: Growth is proving more resilient than many expected, and the bull market remains intact.
key_views: Constructive view on the U.S. economy and stock market.
Falling oil prices reduce recession risks.
AI investment cycle remains a powerful secular force.

commentators.name: Lance Roberts
commentators.key_quote: Investors should expect at least two corrections of at least 5-10% in 2026.
key_views: Market valuations are stretched.
Expect at least two 5-10% corrections in 2026.
Divergence between S&P 500 and median stock performance is a warning.
commentators.price_target_or_view: S&P 500 projected to rise to 8,000 by 2026 (from other sources), but with significant correction risk.

commentators.name: Savita Subramanian
commentators.key_quote: Most of this year's upside from artificial intelligence (AI) surges will result in 'significant multiple compression' next year.
key_views: Expects valuation multiple compression.
AI-heavy stocks are vulnerable.
Recommends overweighting consumer staples and underweighting consumer discretionary.
commentators.price_target_or_view: S&P 500 ending 2026 at 7,100 (most bearish major Wall Street view).

commentators.name: David Rosenberg
commentators.key_quote: The biggest risk ahead isn't volatility; it's misreading this cycle and staying positioned for an economy that no longer exists.
key_views: Markets are propped up by the S&P 500, creating a 'wealth illusion'.
Economy has entered a 'silent' contraction.
Valuation discipline and downside protection are key for 2026.
commentators.price_target_or_view: Warns of a potential recession if the stock market support fades.

commentators.name: Brian Belski
commentators.key_quote: The fundamental backdrop for US equities remains constructive, and investors who chase headlines do so at their own peril.
key_views: Geopolitical events drive short-term volatility, not long-term direction.
Earnings expectations are increasing and Q1 results are constructive.
The market regime is shifting from momentum to fundamentals.
commentators.price_target_or_view: Sees S&P 500 potentially ending the year with an '8 handle' (from other sources).
commentators._validation_warning: Stance auto-corrected: target 500 implies -93.3% downside from current ~7,410, conflicts with 'Bullish' stance.

equity.synthesis: Commentators are divided on the near-term outlook, with a significant split between those who see continued strength driven by earnings and AI, and those who warn of stretched valuations, potential rate hikes, and a looming capital crunch. While some anticipate further upside and a broadening market, others highlight risks of corrections and a potential recession.
themes: AI's impact on earnings and valuations
Inflation and Federal Reserve policy trajectory
Geopolitical risks and their market impact

commentators.name: Michael Saylor
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin goes from 70,000 to 700,000 to $7 million a coin. It's inevitable.
key_views: Bitcoin is digital capital, the foundation for a new financial ecosystem.
Significant room for growth as Bitcoin represents a tiny share of global wealth.
Institutional adoption and new financial products will drive massive price appreciation.
commentators.btc_view: Extremely bullish, projecting up to $7 million per coin.

commentators.name: Peter Schiff
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin is not going to go to zero.
key_views: Conceded Bitcoin will not go to zero, a shift from previous stances.
Continues to criticize Bitcoin's lack of intrinsic value and industrial utility.
Warns of potential price crashes and favors gold as a safe haven.
commentators.btc_view: Acknowledged it won't go to zero, but still highly skeptical of its long-term value and potential for significant price drops.
commentators.price_target: Warns of a plunge below $20,000 if it breaks $50,000.

commentators.name: Arthur Hayes
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin gets thrown out with the bathwater... bearish on nearly every risk asset except large energy producers.
key_views: AI-related debt issuance has drained liquidity, impacting Bitcoin's performance.
Bearish on most risk assets due to potential AI bubble burst.
Believes Bitcoin will experience a broad sell-off if the AI market corrects.
commentators.btc_view: Bearish in the short-to-medium term due to AI liquidity drain and potential market contagion.

commentators.name: Anthony Pompliano
commentators.key_quote: I think more and more people are convinced that that four-year cycle is a real thing.
key_views: The current Bitcoin downturn is one of the healthiest bear markets due to shallower corrections.
The four-year market cycle remains intact.
Bitcoin is a hedge against currency debasement and government spending.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, sees the current dip as a healthy part of the cycle and a buying opportunity.

commentators.name: Cathie Wood
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin is bottoming out, testing a massive base, and preparing for an unstoppable run in the second half of 2026.
key_views: Bitcoin is bottoming out and poised for a significant rally.
The AI boom creates an 'Age of Abundance' that highlights Bitcoin's scarcity.
Institutional flows are steady, and regulatory catalysts are expected.
commentators.btc_view: Very bullish, expects an unstoppable run and sees Bitcoin as a primary investment.
commentators._validation_warning: Stance auto-corrected: target 2,030 implies -96.9% downside from current ~65,748, conflicts with 'Very Bullish' stance.

commentators.name: Raoul Pal
commentators.key_quote: June 2026 is now the most-anticipated month of the year... places their bets on the man who called the last cycle.
key_views: The current cycle mirrors 2017 and could peak in June 2026.
Expects a 5-year supercycle, driving crypto market cap to $100 trillion.
AI, ETFs, and expanding global liquidity are key drivers.
commentators.btc_view: Extremely bullish, predicting a massive surge and new all-time highs.

commentators.name: Mike Novogratz
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin is heading back above $70,000 and that Washington will eventually inflate its way out of debt.
key_views: Bitcoin is likely to reclaim $70,000, especially if the CLARITY Act advances.
The U.S. will likely inflate its way out of its $40 trillion debt.
Cautious about the SpaceX IPO but bullish on Bitcoin's long-term prospects.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, with a 70/30 odds of reclaiming $70,000 tied to regulatory progress.

commentators.name: PlanB
commentators.key_quote: There is a greater than 50% probability that Bitcoin ($BTC) will fall below $61,000.
key_views: There's a significant chance of Bitcoin dropping below $61,000.
Market sentiment is divided, with no definitive signs of a bottom.
Reiterates the Stock-to-Flow model's long-term prediction of $500,000.
commentators.btc_view: Cautious in the short-term, with a high probability of further downside, but maintains a long-term bullish outlook based on S2F model.

commentators.name: Lyn Alden
commentators.key_quote: If all it takes to kill bitcoin is a bullish entity that likes it enough to buy, then 'go home.'
key_views: Defends Bitcoin against the narrative that Strategy's sale could 'kill' it.
Volatility is a defining feature, not a flaw, and contributes to upside potential.
The Bitcoin cycle is driven more by macro factors than the halving.
commentators.btc_view: Structurally bullish, expects new all-time highs and a longer cycle driven by macroeconomics.
commentators.price_target: Expects Bitcoin to be back in the six figures and reach new all-time highs.

commentators.name: Jamie Dimon
commentators.key_quote: We'll fight it... If they don't do it thoughtfully, it'll be a huge problem.
key_views: Strongly opposes the CLARITY Act, viewing it as crypto-friendly and unfair to traditional banks.
Warns stablecoins could become a 'huge problem' and 'blow up'.
Believes crypto should face the same regulations as traditional financial institutions.
commentators.btc_view: Highly skeptical and critical of crypto, particularly stablecoins and the CLARITY Act.
commentators.price_target: Implied negative outlook, warning of potential market 'blow up'.

commentators.name: Tom Lee
commentators.key_quote: AI is delivering real productivity. Companies are cutting costs, output is increasing, and this shift is creating massive winners.
key_views: AI is driving real productivity gains, leading to market expansion.
Bull markets are not killed by old age but by the Fed; Fed policy is not currently restrictive.
The current market is not a bear market but a correction within a larger bull cycle.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, sees the current downturn as a correction within a larger bull cycle, supported by AI productivity.
commentators.price_target: Implied bullishness on continued market expansion.

crypto.synthesis: This week's crypto commentary shows a stark divide between extreme bullishness on Bitcoin's long-term potential, driven by institutional adoption and technological advancements like AI, and deep skepticism rooted in regulatory concerns and macroeconomic instability. While some predict multi-million dollar price targets for Bitcoin, others warn of significant crashes and advocate for traditional safe-haven assets.
themes: AI's impact on liquidity and market dynamics
Institutional adoption and regulatory clarity (CLARITY Act)
Bitcoin's long-term value proposition vs. short-term volatility
Macroeconomic factors (debt, inflation) influencing crypto markets
